Ooha Reddy's Portfolio
Projects Blog About

About Me

Hi, my name is Ooha Reddy ,

I am a fourth-year Computation and Design dual-degree student at Duke Kunshan University in Kunshan, China and Duke University in Durham, NC. While I specialize in 3D object design and VR/AR development, I have experience in incorporating a variety of tools to bring my projects to life, including but not limited to AI/ML/NLP, web development, and UI/UX.

My true strength lies in my resourcefulness and ability to solve unusual problems with out-of-the-box thinking. I do not back down from a challenge, and take every opportunity to understand something differently for the first time.

Currently, I am on a professional development gap year as my program does not offer my major for students graduating prior to 2025. My goal over the next year is to hone my skills the best I can, whether that be through passion projects or professional experience. I hope you enjoy looking through my work!

Skills

  • Java Proficient

  • Data Structures and Algorithms COMPSCI201, Fall 2021

Experience

President, Duke Dance Council

May 2022 — Present

I efficiently managed space reservations and logistics for over 13 dance teams, adhering strictly to Duke's policies. My leadership saw the introduction of programs offering financial support to dance teams, alongside innovative community-focused events such as guest workshops, community showcases, and block battles.

Opinion Managing Editor, Duke Chronicle

January 2021 — August 2023

As an Opinion Managing Editor for Duke Chronicle, I supervised a section with over 30 columnists, refining and publishing more than 100 op-ed columns. My responsibilities also encompassed driving advertising and marketing initiatives for section recruitment each semester.

Director of Racial/Multicultural Diversity and Outreach, Duke Student Government

September 2022 — May 2023

My role as director placed me as the liaison between Duke's cultural groups and Duke Student Government. I led transformative discussions for the 2023 Bryan Center restructuring, aiming to provide dedicated spaces for cultural groups at Duke. Furthermore, I devised and introduced a 90-minute GVPI curriculum to counteract increasing power-based violence at cultural events, in collaboration with the Interim Director of Gender Violence Prevention.

Treasurer, Duke Diya

August 2021 — June 2023

I meticulously handled finances exceeding 100k, ensuring smooth execution of various programs for South Asian-identifying students. My advocacy focused on issues significant to this community, both within the University and the wider Student Affairs realm. I also mentored upcoming treasurers and cultivated relations with local Durham entities to broaden our community impact.

Planktoscope Project Engineer, Marine Microbe Lab

April 2022 — May 2023

In my role as a Planktoscope Project Engineer at the Marine Microbe Lab, I worked on a team of 4 undergraduate students to successfully assemble a Planktoscope prototype running Raspberry Pi OS for the Duke University Marine Lab. This involved conducting over 20 functionality tests at diverse observation sites, including natural marine ecosystems and engineered microalgae systems. Notably, I secured funding through Duke’s Bass Connections Research Program, facilitating further advancements in the Planktoscope project.

Ecological Impact of Rays (AI initiative) Research Intern, Anjali D. Boyd Lab

August 2021 — December 2021

I helped pioneer the development of a Python-based AI program to study stingray abundance on the North Carolina coast using drone footage. This innovative endeavor required me to adapt the existing OysterNet AI model, enabling precise detection and mathematical modeling of stingray population dynamics. Collaborative efforts with the interdisciplinary teams of the ADB group played a pivotal role in augmenting our model's functionality.

Senior Editor, Herff Jones Publishing

July 2021 — July 2022

My tenure as a Senior Editor at Herff Jones Publishing was marked by facilitating a comprehensive workshop, attended by over 900 high school students. The workshop emphasized graphic theme creation, copywriting, and marketing tactics. I personally assisted individual teams in devising unique yearbook designs and initiated a special seminar tailored for low-income students. This seminar focused on graphic arts education via free online tools.

Communications Specialist, Duke University School of Nursing

August 2021 — May 2022

At the Duke University School of Nursing, I significantly enhanced communication channels for the HEEAT Lab, dedicated to analyzing health disparities, especially in HIV treatment for Black women. My primary contribution was formulating and executing strategies to advocate for PrEP treatment in marginalized US communities, primarily utilizing visual media channels.

Director of Racial/Multicultural Diversity and Outreach, Duke Student Government

September 2022 — May 2023

My role as director placed me as the liaison between Duke's cultural groups and Duke Student Government. I led transformative discussions for the 2023 Bryan Center restructuring, aiming to provide dedicated spaces for cultural groups at Duke. Furthermore, I devised and introduced a 90-minute GVPI curriculum to counteract increasing power-based violence at cultural events, in collaboration with the Interim Director of Gender Violence Prevention.

President, Duke Dance Council

May 2022 — Present

I efficiently managed space reservations and logistics for over 13 dance teams, adhering strictly to Duke's policies. My leadership saw the introduction of programs offering financial support to dance teams, alongside innovative community-focused events such as guest workshops, community showcases, and block battles.

Treasurer, Duke Diya

August 2021 — June 2023

I meticulously handled finances exceeding $100k, ensuring smooth execution of various programs for South Asian-identifying students. My advocacy focused on issues significant to the community, both within the University and the wider Student Affairs realm. I also mentored upcoming treasurers and cultivated relations with local Durham entities to broaden our community impact.

Digital Strategist, DukeStudents Social Media

January 2021 — May 2022

Working as a Digital Strategist for @DukeStudents, I supported a robust team of editors and content creators in portraying a positive image of Duke University to a vast online audience. My duties ranged from brainstorming content to ensuring its consistency and quality across various platforms.

Research Fellow, Lab for Social Choreography

August 2021 — May 2022

As a Research Fellow at the Lab for Social Choreography, I was part of an interdisciplinary team of 12 students, blending statistical sciences, sociology, and performing arts. Our primary mission was to gauge the effectiveness of performing arts in integrating displaced populations, both in Paris and North Carolina. The project aimed to create a digital platform to centralize artistic expression in target communities.

Editor-in-Chief, Grizzly Legacy Yearbook

February 2018 — June 2020

Leading as the Editor-in-Chief for Grizzly Legacy Yearbook, I oversaw a dynamic team of 30, producing over 300 content pages in a systematic manner. With a financial oversight of more than $100k, I successfully published six yearbooks, two of which were recognized nationally in Herff Jones’ "Portfolio."
